notes: Baseline-B: FP32 tiled model, all tile operands/results access DRAM; no BRAM reuse. Uses LayerNorm-style multi-pass FP32 normalization.
Baseline-B: FP32 tiled model, all tile operands/results access DRAM; no BRAM reuse.
Baseline-B: FP32 tiled model, all tile operands/results access DRAM; no BRAM reuse.
Baseline-B: FP32 tiled model, all tile operands/results access DRAM; no BRAM reuse. Uses standard FP32 softmax; exp/division modeled as elementwise operations, not LUT.
Baseline-B: FP32 tiled model, all tile operands/results access DRAM; no BRAM reuse.
Baseline-B: FP32 tiled model, all tile operands/results access DRAM; no BRAM reuse.
Baseline-B: FP32 tiled model, all tile operands/results access DRAM; no BRAM reuse. No FC1+GELU fusion: hidden activation is materialized in DRAM.
Optimized INT8 hardware-aware model with maximum weight reuse loop ordering. LayerNorm is replaced by streaming RMSNorm with inv-rms/gamma buffers and LUT-like reciprocal sqrt support.

Optimized INT8 hardware-aware model with maximum weight reuse loop ordering. W_qkv tile [384,16] is loaded once per N-tile and reused across all token M-tiles. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.

Optimized INT8 hardware-aware model with maximum weight reuse loop ordering. Q/K/V remain on-chip; current-head INT32 score buffer is materialized in shared intermediate BRAM. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.
Optimized INT8 hardware-aware model with maximum weight reuse loop ordering. Score is read from BRAM, exp is replaced by LUT, and INT8 attention overwrites score space per head.

Optimized INT8 hardware-aware model with maximum weight reuse loop ordering. A_h and V_h are read from BRAM; O_h is written directly into O_attn layout. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.

Optimized INT8 hardware-aware model with maximum weight reuse loop ordering. W_o tile [384,16] is loaded once per output-channel tile and reused across all token M-tiles. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.

Optimized INT8 hardware-aware model with maximum weight reuse loop ordering. FC1+GELU+requant fusion avoids FP32 hidden materialization; W_fc1/W_fc2 tiles are reused across all M-tiles. Ping-pong uses max(compute, DRAM preload, BRAM service) as the latency lower bound.
